# programs.pi-coding-agent

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `programs.pi-coding-agent`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `programs.pi-coding-agent.configDir` | `string` | Directory holding Pi Coding Agent's configuration files. Defaults to {file}`~/.pi/agent`, matching the upstream {command}`pi` CLI default. The {env}`PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR` environment variable is exported automatically whenever the directory differs from this default so the CLI reads configuration from the same location. |
| `programs.pi-coding-agent.context` | `strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path` | Global context for Pi Coding Agent. The value is either: - Inline content as a string - A path to a file containing the content The configured content is written to {file}`AGENTS.md` inside {option}`programs.pi-coding-agent.configDir` (default {file}`~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md`). |
| `programs.pi-coding-agent.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable pi-coding-agent. |
| `programs.pi-coding-agent.extraPackages` | `list of package` | Extra packages available to Pi Coding Agent. These are added to the PATH of the wrapped pi binary. Needed for packages installed by pi (e.g. {command}`npm:@termdraw/pi` requires {command}`npm` and {command}`bun`). |
| `programs.pi-coding-agent.keybindings` | `JSON value` | Keybindings configuration written to {file}`~/.pi/agent/keybindings.json`. See <https://pi.dev/docs/latest/keybindings> for the documentation. |
| `programs.pi-coding-agent.models` | `JSON value` | Custom model providers written to {file}`~/.pi/agent/models.json`. Each provider entry may contain `baseUrl`, `api`, `apiKey`, `compat`, and a `models` list with `id`, `name`, `reasoning`, etc. See <https://pi.dev/docs/latest/models> for the documentation. |
| `programs.pi-coding-agent.package` | `null or package` | The pi-coding-agent package to use. |
| `programs.pi-coding-agent.settings` | `JSON value` | Configuration written to {file}`~/.pi/agent/settings.json`. See <https://pi.dev/docs/latest/settings> for the documentation. |
